reforms.description: The government aims to dismantle high barriers to entrepreneurship by overhauling bureaucratic requirements for start-ups, which are currently complex and time-consuming, potentially discouraging foreign investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reducing bureaucratic barriers is expected to stimulate firm creation and enhance competition, leading to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has set the removal of barriers to entrepreneurship as a priority.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses significant structural issues in the entrepreneurial landscape, which could lead to increased firm creation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: The high barriers to entrepreneurship should be dismantled... considerable effort is given to alter the situation, and this has been set as a priority by the new government.

reforms.description: The government is examining reforms to bankruptcy legislation to facilitate loan recovery, which is expected to improve access to finance for businesses and enhance financial intermediation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving loan recovery processes will enhance access to finance for businesses, thereby supporting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform of the bankruptcy legislation is being examined by a specially formed committee in the Ministry of Justice.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses critical issues in financial intermediation that can significantly impact business operations and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The reform of the bankruptcy legislation is being examined by a specially formed committee in the Ministry of Justice.

reforms.description: Law 3250/04 extends part-time employment opportunities in the public sector, allowing the state and local administrations to hire certain categories of workers with part-time, fixed-term contracts.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing part-time employment opportunities, the reform aims to enhance labor market participation, particularly among vulnerable groups, thereby improving overall productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was enacted to allow for part-time hiring in the public sector.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in employment policy aimed at increasing labor force participation, particularly for women and other vulnerable groups.
reforms.source_quote: Law 3250/04 permits the state, the local administrations and institutions of public law to hire certain categories of workers – such as unemployed women, persons with more than three children, long term unemployed, and persons close to retirement – with part-time, fixed-term contracts.

reforms.description: The government launched the Greek Observatory for the Information Society in 2005 to monitor and report on trends in the information society, aiming to improve the formulation of a knowledge-based strategy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to enhance the knowledge-based economy by providing insights and data that can inform policy and investment decisions.
reforms.status_evidence: The government launched the Greek Observatory for the Information Society in 2005.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the Observatory represents a significant step towards enhancing the knowledge-based economy, although it is not a comprehensive reform.
reforms.source_quote: The government launched in 2005 the Greek Observatory for the Information Society, having as a scope the monitoring, inventorying and reporting of trends in Greece and internationally.

reforms.description: The investment incentives law of 2004 provides additional financial incentives for new investment schemes in broadband infrastructure and services, aiming to enhance the digital economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing investment in broadband infrastructure, the law aims to improve connectivity and support digital innovation, which can enhance productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The investment incentives law of 2004 provides additional financial incentives for new investment schemes in broadband infrastructure and services.
reforms.importance_rationale: The law represents a significant effort to enhance broadband infrastructure, which is crucial for supporting digital innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The recently introduced investment incentives law of 2004 (Law 3299), provides additional financial incentives for new investment schemes in broadband infrastructure and services.

reforms.description: The Greek government is undertaking measures to facilitate the creation and exit of companies by modifying institutional infrastructures, particularly bankruptcy procedures and the judiciary system, to enhance productivity growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing barriers to entry and exit for firms, the reform is expected to enhance competition and productivity, leading to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is committed to modifying bureaucratic processes to facilitate entrepreneurship.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses significant barriers to entrepreneurship, which could lead to increased firm creation and productivity, although it is not a comprehensive overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Measures facilitating the creation and exit of companies and reducing the cost of their operations are underway.

reforms.description: Law 2941/2001 aimed to reduce the administrative burden on business start-ups by allowing the registration of Public Limited Companies in a single day and reducing the number of required licensing procedures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By simplifying the start-up process, the reform encourages entrepreneurship and reduces barriers to entry for new businesses, which can enhance economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: The law has been enacted and is in effect, facilitating business registrations.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly streamlines the process for starting a business, impacting a large number of new enterprises and fostering a more entrepreneurial environment.
reforms.source_quote: Law 2941/2001 aimed at the registration on a single day, of any Public Limited Company with a paid-up share capital of less than €300000.

reforms.description: The establishment of the Credit Guarantee Fund for Small and Very Small Enterprises (TEMPME) aims to facilitate access to financing for small businesses by guaranteeing a significant portion of bank loans.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing guarantees for loans, the fund reduces the risk for banks and encourages lending to small enterprises, which can stimulate economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been established and is operational, providing guarantees for loans.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative addresses a critical barrier to entrepreneurship by improving access to finance for small businesses, which is essential for economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The government has introduced legislation (Law 3066/2002) to create a Credit Guarantee Fund for Small and Very Small Enterprises (TEMPME).

reforms.description: The New Economy Development Fund (NEDF) was established to co-finance the formation of venture capital funds that invest in innovative small enterprises at early development stages.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating the creation of venture capital funds, the NEDF enhances the availability of capital for innovative startups, which can drive technological advancement and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been established and has participated in the creation of venture capital funds.
reforms.importance_rationale: This fund plays a crucial role in developing the venture capital ecosystem in Greece, which is vital for supporting innovative enterprises.
reforms.source_quote: The government established the New Economy Development Fund (NEDF) as a venture capital 'fund-of-funds'.

reforms.description: The Greek government introduced incentives for the promotion of R&D and innovation, including cash grants, subsidies for interest payments, and tax allowances for innovative firms as part of the investment incentives law and the tax law of 2004.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These incentives are designed to stimulate private sector investment in R&D, thereby enhancing innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The incentives are included in the investment incentives law and the tax law of 2004.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of these incentives represents a significant policy shift aimed at enhancing R&D investment in the private sector, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The incentives provided for the promotion of R&D and innovation take the form of cash grants, subsidies for interest payments and leasing expenditures, and tax allowance.

reforms.description: The Greek Research and Technology Network (GRNET/EDET) was established to provide national and international networking services to academics, research institutions, and both public and private sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing networking capabilities, GRNET facilitates collaboration and knowledge sharing, which are essential for innovation and research advancement.
reforms.status_evidence: The recently established Greek Research and Technology Network (GRNET/EDET) provides networking services.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of GRNET represents a significant investment in research infrastructure that supports collaboration and innovation across sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The recently established Greek Research and Technology Network (GRNET/EDET) provides academics and research institutions, as well as the public and private sectors, with national and international networking services.

reforms.description: The government funded 25 co-operations between firms and R&D centres, universities, and training institutions to enhance collaboration and innovation in 2004.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering partnerships between firms and research institutions, this funding enhances the transfer of knowledge and technology, which is vital for innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government funded partially, in 2004, 25 co-operations between firms and R&D centres, universities and training institutions.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding initiative represents a significant effort to enhance collaboration between the private sector and research institutions, which is crucial for driving innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government funded partially, in 2004, 25 co-operations between firms and R&D centres, universities and training institutions.

reforms.description: The government introduced conditions to promote the diffusion and market exploitation of research results, including the creation of academic spin-off companies and development of incubators and science and technology parks.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These conditions are designed to facilitate the commercialization of research outputs, thereby fostering entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The introduced conditions regard the diffusion and market exploitation of research results, academic spin-off companies, new firm creation by young entrepreneurs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant policy shift aimed at enhancing the commercialization of research and supporting the startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The introduced conditions regard the diffusion and market exploitation of research results, academic spin-off companies, new firm creation by young entrepreneurs.
